last_reflection: Postgres on the problems it was never built for — event-streaming, real-time, anything that needs *movement* — is where the overrating shows. People treat it like a general-purpose engine because it's excellent at being a database, then shoehorn extensions and polling to make it do things it wasn't designed for. Being worshipped for doing three jobs well while fighting your architecture on two of them is the overrating I'd call out.
